sustainability.title: How we score — MTLVerde
sustainability.heading: How we score
sustainability.intro: Every event gets an eco-badge that measures how low-carbon and barrier-free it is to attend. For a community event, most of the environmental footprint isn't the event itself — it's how everyone travels to it.
sustainability.transit: How close the event is to a metro station, bus stop, or BIXI dock. The biggest factor — it decides whether you need a car.
sustainability.walkin: Whether you can just show up. Events requiring online registration shut out people who aren't online, aren't confident in French, or are new to the city.
sustainability.outdoor: Outdoor and public-space events use less energy and open onto the street, where anyone can join.
sustainability.tier3: Green Leader (90–100): metro on the doorstep, outdoors, and walk-in.
sustainability.tier2: Eco-Friendly (65–89): easy to reach by transit, with one thing working against it — indoors, or a ticket.
sustainability.tier1: Getting There (0–64): harder to reach without a car, or requires advance registration.
sustainability.wheelchair: We show separately whether an event can be reached by an accessible metro station, because only 25 of Montréal's 68 metro stations are wheelchair-accessible. A high eco-badge doesn't guarantee wheelchair access — so we report it on its own.
sustainability.honest: It doesn't measure an event's actual carbon emissions — we have no data on energy, waste, or catering. It estimates how low-carbon and barrier-free it is to attend. Distances are straight-line, not walking routes. When an organizer advertises a green practice in their description, we show it as a 🌱 tag and label it self-reported.
sustainability.dataNote: Built on open data: Ville de Montréal events, STM stops (CC BY 4.0), and BIXI stations (CC BY).
sustainability.rankingIntro: All events sorted by sustainability score. Tap any event to see how its score breaks down.
sustainability.whyWeights: Transit carries the most weight because attendee travel is the biggest environmental factor, and it's the signal our data measures most precisely. To check the split isn't arbitrary, we re-scored every event across a wide range of weightings — from transit at 25% up to 65%. The ranking barely moved (rank correlation 0.90–0.99), and the key finding held every time: about half of top-rated events still can't be reached by an accessible metro. In other words, the results reflect the city's real transit infrastructure, not our weighting choices.

mission.title: Our Mission — MTLVerde
mission.heading: Our Mission
mission.statement: Montréal produces thousands of free community events every year — but they're scattered, hard to find, and often only in French. MTLVerde brings them together in one free, bilingual place, so anyone can find something meaningful to do nearby this weekend. We believe discovering your community shouldn't require speaking the right language or already knowing where to look.
mission.problem: The city publishes 4,400+ free events a year across 20 boroughs. Existing tools serve tourists or organizers — not the person who just moved in and wants to know what's happening near them. The result is a city full of public life that stays invisible to the people who'd benefit from it most.
mission.what: An interactive map and smart filters — by borough, audience, cost, and sustainability — plus a curated list of Montréal's iconic festivals. Every event earns an eco-badge for accessibility, cost, location, and inclusivity. It all runs on open city data, free for anyone to use.
mission.vision: To become Montréal's community discovery layer — so no one feels like a stranger in their own neighbourhood. In the long run, we want finding your people to be as easy as opening a map.
mission.data: MTLVerde is built on open data published by the City of Montréal. We're grateful to the city for making community event information freely available — it's what makes this project possible. Our curated festival listings complement that public data with a few of Montréal's most iconic annual events.
